mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns do not significantly exceed the historical baseline when FEDFUNDS declines for three consecutive months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving upward earnings revisions that lift equity prices over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.5% within a single calendar month.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, driving corporate profit margin expansion and pushing equity returns higher over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between the joint INDPRO-HOUST composite signal and 60-day SPX returns remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflationary environment where inflation rises alongside slowing growth, equity valuations contract rapidly as real discount rates spike and earnings growth stalls, driving negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL rises while GDPC1 growth decelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic resilience, supporting consumer confidence and corporate revenue expectations, which drives positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with sequential PAYEMS growth (Pearson r < 0.10 over a 5-year sample).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 3.5% annualized, aggregate demand outpaces supply constraints, triggering earnings beat cycles that sustain positive equity momentum over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid federal funds rate increases combined with rising unemployment signal a policy-induced demand shock that compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward returns over a one-month window.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.02 when FEDFUNDS rises and UNRATE increases simultaneously, indicating the joint signal fails to predict negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain disruption phases, sharp declines in industrial production reduce corporate throughput and margin expectations, causing immediate equity sell-offs over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO falls for two consecutive months.
